feelings.name: Frustrated
feelings.text: I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.

feelings.name: Annoyed
feelings.text: A fool gives full vent to his spirit, but a wise man quietly holds it back.

feelings.name: Jealous
feelings.text: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there will be disorder and every vile practice.

feelings.name: Hurt
feelings.text: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.

feelings.name: Critical
feelings.text: Let no corrupting talk come out of your mouths, but only such as is good for building up, as fits the occasion, that it may give grace to those who hear.

feelings.name: Disappointed
feelings.text: And hope does not put us to shame, because God's love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who has been given to us.

feelings.name: Confident
feelings.text: I can do all things through him who strengthens me.

feelings.name: Valued
feelings.text: Why, even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not; you are of more value than many sparrows.

feelings.name: Courageous
feelings.text: Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be in dread of them, for it is the LORD your God who goes with you. He will not leave you or forsake you.

feelings.name: Capable
feelings.text: Not that we are sufficient in ourselves to claim anything as coming from us, but our sufficiency is from God.

feelings.name: Respected
feelings.text: Let no one despise you for your youth, but set the believers an example in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.

feelings.name: Secure
feelings.text: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous man runs into it and is safe.

feelings.name: Peaceful
feelings.text: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.

feelings.name: Thoughtful
feelings.text: Finally, brothers, whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is just, whatever is pure, whatever is lovely, whatever is commendable, if there is any excellence, if there is anything worthy of praise, think about these things.

feelings.name: Trusting
feelings.text: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.

feelings.name: Satisfied
feelings.text: For he satisfies the longing soul, and the hungry soul he fills with good things.

feelings.name: Content
feelings.text: Keep your life free from love of money, and be content with what you have, for he has said, 'I will never leave you nor forsake you.'

feelings.name: Resting
feelings.text: In peace I will both lie down and sleep; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.

feelings.name: Joyful
feelings.text: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.

feelings.name: Excited
feelings.text: This is the day that the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.

feelings.name: Hopeful
feelings.text: For I know the plans I have for you, declares the LORD, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.

feelings.name: Inspired
feelings.text: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them.

feelings.name: Cheerful
feelings.text: A glad heart makes a cheerful face, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is crushed.

feelings.name: Grateful
feelings.text: Give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.

feelings.name: Lonely
feelings.text: Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.

feelings.name: Overwhelmed
feelings.text: From the end of the earth I call to you when my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

feelings.name: Ashamed
feelings.text: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

feelings.name: Left Out
feelings.text: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the LORD will take me in.

feelings.name: Bored
feelings.text: Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men.

feelings.name: Tired
feelings.text: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.

feelings.name: Anxious
feelings.text: Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.

feelings.name: Worried
feelings.text: Therefore do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.

feelings.name: Insecure
feelings.text: I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Wonderful are your works; my soul knows it very well.

feelings.name: Scared
feelings.text: Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.

feelings.name: Helpless
feelings.text: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

feelings.name: Nervous
feelings.text: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.

feelings.name: Amazed
feelings.text: You are the God who works wonders; you have made known your might among the peoples.

feelings.name: Startled
feelings.text: If you lie down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.

feelings.name: Awestruck
feelings.text: Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him!

feelings.name: Confused
feelings.text: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding.

feelings.name: Shocked
feelings.text: For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, declares the LORD.

feelings.name: Wonder
feelings.text: Hear this, O Job; stop and consider the wondrous works of God.

feelings.name: Judgmental
feelings.text: Judge not, that you be not judged. For with the judgment you pronounce you will be judged, and with the measure you use it will be measured to you.

feelings.name: Disapproving
feelings.text: Let love be genuine. Abhor what is evil; hold fast to what is good.

feelings.name: Bitter
feelings.text: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice. Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you.

feelings.name: Avoidant
feelings.text: Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not walk in the way of the evil. Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.

feelings.name: Awful
feelings.text: My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

feelings.name: Hesitant
feelings.text: If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given him.
